title: Uncertainty-Aware Diagnostics for Physics-Informed Machine Learning
abstract: Physics-informed machine learning (PIML) integrates prior physical information, often in the form of differential equation constraints, into the process of fitting ML models to physical data. Popular PIML approaches, including neural operators, physics-informed neural networks, and neural ordinary differential equations, are typically fit to objectives that simultaneously include both data and physical constraints. However, the multi-objective nature of this approach creates ambiguity in the measurement of model quality. This is related to a poor understanding of epistemic uncertainty, and it can lead to surprising failure modes, even when existing metrics suggest strong fits. Working within a Gaussian process regression framework, we introduce the Physics-Informed Log Evidence (PILE) score. Bypassing the ambiguities of test losses, the PILE score is a single, uncertainty-aware metric that provides a selection principle for hyperparameters of a physics-informed model. We show that PILE minimization yields excellent choices for a wide variety of model parameters, including kernel bandwidth, least squares regularization weights, and even kernel function selection. We also show that, prior to data acquisition, a special data-free case of the PILE score identifies a-priori kernel choices that are "well adapted" to a given PDE. Beyond the kernel setting, we anticipate that the PILE score can be extended to PIML at large, and we outline approaches to do so.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有PIML方法依赖数据与物理方程的联合损失，但这种多目标优化使模型评估不明确。
  - 缺乏对认知不确定性（epistemic uncertainty）的理解，导致模型看似拟合良好却实际失败。
  - 现有指标无法有效指导超参数选择，影响模型可靠性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出“物理信息对数证据”（PILE）评分，基于高斯过程回归框架。
  - PILE是一个单一、考虑不确定性的评估指标，能同时衡量数据拟合与物理一致性。
  - 通过最小化PILE来自动选择超参数，如核函数、带宽、正则化权重等。
  - 可在无数据情况下使用（数据无关版本），提前筛选适合特定偏微分方程的核函数。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）

title: $p\textrm{-less}$ Sampling: A Robust Hyperparameter-Free Approach for LLM Decoding
abstract:
Obtaining high-quality outputs from Large Language Models (LLMs) often depends upon the choice of a sampling-based decoding strategy to probabilistically choose the next token at each generation step. While a variety of such sampling methods have been proposed, their performance can be sensitive to the selection of hyperparameters which may require different settings depending upon the generation task and temperature configuration. In this work, we introduce $p\textrm{-less}$ sampling: an information-theoretic approach to sampling which dynamically sets a truncation threshold at each decoding step based on the entire token probability distribution. Unlike existing methods, $p\textrm{-less}$ sampling has no hyperparameters and consistently produces high-quality outputs as temperature increases. We provide theoretical perspectives on $p$-less sampling to ground our proposed method and conduct experiments to empirically validate its effectiveness across a range of math, logical reasoning, and creative writing tasks. Our results demonstrate how $p\textrm{-less}$ sampling consistently outperforms existing sampling approaches while exhibiting much less degradation in text quality at higher temperature values. We further show how $p$-less achieves greater inference-time efficiency than alternative methods through lower average token sampling times and shorter generation lengths, without sacrificing accuracy.
Finally, we provide analyses to highlight the benefits of $p\textrm{-less}$ through qualitative examples, case studies, and diversity assessments.

title: Post-hoc Probabilistic Vision-Language Models
abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs), such as CLIP and SigLIP, have found remarkable success in classification, retrieval, and generative tasks. For this, VLMs deterministically map images and text descriptions to a joint latent space in which their similarity is assessed using the cosine similarity. However, a deterministic mapping of inputs fails to capture uncertainties over concepts arising from domain shifts when used in downstream tasks. In this work, we propose post-hoc uncertainty estimation in VLMs that does not require additional training. Our method leverages a Bayesian posterior approximation over the last layers in VLMs and analytically quantifies uncertainties over cosine similarities. We demonstrate its effectiveness for uncertainty quantification and support set selection in active learning. Compared to baselines, we obtain improved and well-calibrated predictive uncertainties, interpretable uncertainty estimates, and sample-efficient active learning. Our results show promise for safety-critical applications of large-scale models.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何在不重新训练的前提下，为视觉语言模型（VLMs）提供可靠的不确定性估计。  

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有VLMs（如CLIP）采用确定性映射，无法捕捉因领域变化带来的概念不确定性，影响下游任务可靠性。  
  - 在安全关键场景（如医疗、自动驾驶）中，模型需能“知道自己不知道”，但现有方法缺乏有效不确定性量化手段。  
  - 传统不确定性估计需重新训练或修改模型结构，成本高且不灵活。  

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出后处理（post-hoc）方法，仅在VLM的最后几层引入贝叶斯近似，无需重新训练。  
  - 通过分析性推导，直接计算图像与文本在嵌入空间中余弦相似度的不确定性。  
  - 利用该不确定性进行主动学习中的样本选择，提升学习效率。  

- 结论是什么？  
  - 在不确定性量化和主动学习任务中，优于现有基线方法，预测不确定性更准确且校准良好。  
  - 无需训练，即可获得可解释的不确定性估计，显著提升样本效率。  
  - 适用于大规模VLMs，为安全关键应用提供可靠保障。  

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次提出无需训练的后处理不确定性估计框架，适用于已部署的VLMs。  
  - 通过解析方法直接建模余弦相似度的不确定性，避免复杂采样或近似。  
  - 实现了可解释性与实用性的统一，推动VLMs在高风险场景落地。

title: Rethinking Uncertainty Estimation in LLMs: A Principled Single-Sequence Measure
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly employed in real-world applications, driving the need to evaluate the trustworthiness of their generated text. To this end, reliable uncertainty estimation is essential. Leading uncertainty estimation methods generate and analyze multiple output sequences, which is computationally expensive and impractical at scale. In this work, we inspect the theoretical foundations of these methods and explore new directions to enhance computational efficiency. Building on the framework of proper scoring rules, we find that the negative log-likelihood of the most likely output sequence constitutes a theoretically principled uncertainty measure. To approximate this alternative measure, we propose G-NLL, obtained using a single output sequence from greedy decoding. This approach streamlines uncertainty estimation while preserving theoretical rigor. Empirical results demonstrate that G-NLL achieves state-of-the-art performance across various scenarios. Our work lays the theoretical foundation for efficient and reliable uncertainty estimation in natural language generation, challenging the necessity of the prevalent methods that are more complex and resource-intensive.

- 结论：G-NLL在多种任务中表现优于现有方法，计算效率极高，适用于大规模实际部署。

title: Semantic Uncertainty Quantification of Hallucinations in LLMs: A Quantum Tensor Network Based Method
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong generative capabilities but remain vulnerable to confabulations, fluent yet unreliable outputs that vary arbitrarily even under identical prompts. Leveraging a quantum tensor network–based pipeline, we propose a quantum physics-inspired uncertainty quantification framework that accounts for the aleatoric uncertainty in token sequence probability for semantic equivalence-based clustering of LLM generations. In turn, this offers a principled and interpretable scheme for hallucination detection. We further introduce an entropy-maximization strategy that prioritizes high-certainty, semantically coherent outputs and highlights entropy regions where LLM decisions are likely to be unreliable, offering practical guidelines for when human oversight is warranted. We evaluate the robustness of our scheme under different generation lengths and quantization levels, dimensions overlooked in prior studies, demonstrating that our approach remains reliable even in resource-constrained deployments. A total of 116 experiments on TriviaQA, NQ, SVAMP, and SQuAD across multiple architectures (Mistral-7B, Mistral-7B-instruct, Falcon-rw-1b, LLaMA-3.2-1b, LLaMA-2-13b-chat, LLaMA-2-7b-chat, LLaMA-2-13b and LLaMA-2-7b) show consistent improvements in AUROC and AURAC over state-of-the-art baselines.

  - 大语言模型虽能生成流畅文本，但常产生不可靠的“幻觉”输出，现有方法难以准确判断哪些输出可信，缺乏可解释的不确定性评估机制。
  - 在医疗、法律等高风险场景中，必须知道模型何时“不确定”，才能决定是否需要人工干预。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 借鉴量子张量网络（Quantum Tensor Network）建模语言生成过程中的语义不确定性。

  - 引入熵最大化策略，突出模型决策最不可靠的区域，帮助判断何时应人工介入。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）

title: Supporting High-Stakes Decision Making Through Interactive Preference Elicitation in the Latent Space
abstract:
High-stakes, infrequent consumer decisions, such as housing selection, challenge conventional recommender systems due to sparse interaction signals, heterogeneous multi-criteria objectives, and high-dimensional feature spaces. 
This work presents an interactive preference elicitation framework that couples preferential Bayesian optimization (PBO) with two complementary components: (i) large language models (LLMs) that interpret natural language input to produce personalized probabilistic priors over feature utility weights to mitigate cold start, and (ii) an autoencoder (AE)-based latent representation that reduces effective dimensionality for sample-efficient exploration. The framework learns a latent utility function from user pairwise comparisons observed and integrated in real-time.
We evaluate the developed method on rental real estate datasets from two major European cities. The results show that executing PBO in an AE latent space improves final pairwise ranking accuracy by 12%. For LLM-based preference prior generation, we find that direct, LLM-driven weight specification is outperformed by a static prior, while probabilistic weight priors that use LLMs only to rank feature importance achieve 25% better pairwise accuracy on average than a direct approach.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 针对高风险、低频的消费决策（如租房），如何在用户互动中高效获取其偏好，以提升推荐准确性。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统推荐系统在租房等高价值决策中因用户交互少、偏好复杂、特征维度高而效果差。
  - 用户难以直接表达偏好，需通过自然语言或简单比较来逐步揭示真实需求。
  - 需要一种能结合语言理解与高效探索的交互式推荐机制。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 采用偏好贝叶斯优化（PBO）在潜在空间中搜索最优推荐。
  - 使用自动编码器（AE）将高维特征降维至低维潜在空间，提升探索效率。
  - 利用大语言模型（LLM）解析用户自然语言输入，生成特征重要性排序与概率化偏好先验。
  - 实时收集用户对推荐对的偏好选择，动态更新潜在效用函数。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在欧洲两个城市的租房数据集上，PBO在AE潜在空间中使最终推荐准确率提升12%。
  - LLM生成的特征重要性排序+概率先验，比直接指定权重提升25%的准确率。
  - 适用于高价值、低频、多维度决策场景，如租房、购车、医疗选择。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）

  - 构建端到端交互式框架，融合语言理解、降维建模与实时偏好学习。

title: Uncertainty as Feature Gaps: Epistemic Uncertainty Quantification of LLMs in Contextual Question-Answering
abstract: Uncertainty Quantification (UQ) research has primarily focused on closed-book factual question answering (QA), while contextual QA remains unexplored, despite its importance in real-world applications. In this work, we focus on UQ for the contextual QA task and propose a theoretically grounded approach to quantify \emph{epistemic uncertainty}. We begin by introducing a task-agnostic, token-level uncertainty measure defined as the cross-entropy between the predictive distribution of the given model and the unknown true distribution. By decomposing this measure, we isolate the epistemic component and approximate the true distribution by a perfectly prompted, idealized model. We then derive an upper bound for epistemic uncertainty and show that it can be interpreted as semantic feature gaps in the given model’s hidden representations relative to the ideal model. We further apply this generic framework to the contextual QA task and hypothesize that three features approximate this gap: \emph{context-reliance} (using the provided context rather than parametric knowledge), \emph{context comprehension} (extracting relevant information from context), and \emph{honesty} (avoiding intentional lies). Using a top-down interpretability approach, we extract these features by using only a small number of labeled samples and ensemble them to form a robust uncertainty score. Experiments on multiple QA benchmarks in both in-distribution and out-of-distribution settings show that our method substantially outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised (sampling-free and sampling-based) and supervised UQ methods, achieving up to a 13-point PRR improvement while incurring a negligible inference overhead.

title: HOTA: Hamiltonian framework for Optimal Transport Advection
abstract: Optimal transport (OT) has become a natural framework for guiding the probability flows. Yet, the majority of recent generative models assume trivial geometry (e.g., Euclidean) and rely on strong density-estimation assumptions, yielding trajectories that do not respect the true principles of optimality in the underlying manifold. We present Hamiltonian Optimal Transport Advection (HOTA), a Hamilton–Jacobi–Bellman based method that tackles the dual dynamical OT problem explicitly through Kantorovich potentials, enabling efficient and scalable trajectory optimization. Our approach effectively evades the need for explicit density modeling, performing even when the cost functionals are non-smooth. Empirically, HOTA outperforms all baselines in standard benchmarks, as well as in custom datasets with non-differentiable costs, both in terms of feasibility and optimality.
explain_zh:
- 问题：现有生成模型在复杂几何空间中难以生成最优路径，因依赖简单几何假设和密度估计。
- 动机：真实数据分布常存在于复杂流形上，传统方法无法准确捕捉最优传输路径。
- 方法：基于哈密顿-雅可比-贝尔曼方程，用坎托罗维奇势函数显式求解双动态最优传输问题，避免显式建模密度。
- 结论：HOTA在标准和自定义数据集上均优于基线，尤其在非光滑代价函数下表现优异。
- 创新点：首次将哈密顿框架引入最优传输，实现无需密度估计的高效轨迹优化。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在复杂几何空间（如非欧几里得流形）中生成符合最优传输原理的高质量概率路径。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现实数据（如蛋白质结构、社交网络）常分布在复杂几何结构上，传统生成模型假设简单几何（如平直空间），且依赖密度估计，导致生成路径不真实、不最优。  
- 为实现真正“最优”的概率流动，需摆脱对密度建模的依赖，直接从传输原理出发。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出HOTA框架，基于哈密顿-雅可比-贝尔曼（HJB）方程求解双动态最优传输问题。  
- 核心是利用坎托罗维奇势函数显式表示最优路径，无需显式建模概率密度。  
- 通过变分优化求解势函数，实现高效、可扩展的轨迹生成，支持非光滑代价函数。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在标准生成任务（如MNIST、CelebA）和自定义非光滑代价数据集上，HOTA均优于现有方法。  
- 在路径可行性与最优性上表现更优，尤其在复杂或不规则代价下仍稳定有效。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将哈密顿框架引入最优传输，构建了无需密度估计的轨迹优化新范式。  
- 通过坎托罗维奇势函数直接建模最优路径，突破传统生成模型对密度建模的依赖。

title: COMPASS: Robust Feature Conformal Prediction for Medical Segmentation Metrics
abstract: In clinical applications, the utility of segmentation models is often based on the accuracy of derived downstream metrics such as organ size, rather than by the pixel-level accuracy of the segmentation masks themselves. Thus, uncertainty quantification for such metrics is crucial for decision-making. Conformal prediction (CP) is a popular framework to derive such principled uncertainty guarantees, but applying CP naively to the final scalar metric is inefficient because it treats the complex, non-linear segmentation-to-metric pipeline as a black box. We introduce COMPASS, a practical framework that generates efficient, metric-based CP intervals for image segmentation models by leveraging the inductive biases of their underlying deep neural networks. COMPASS performs calibration directly in the model's representation space by perturbing intermediate features along low-dimensional subspaces maximally sensitive to the target metric. We prove that COMPASS achieves valid marginal coverage under the assumption of exchangeability. Empirically, we demonstrate that COMPASS produces significantly tighter intervals than traditional CP baselines on four medical image segmentation tasks for area estimation of skin lesions and anatomical structures. Furthermore, we show that leveraging learned internal features to estimate importance weights allows COMPASS to also recover target coverage under covariate shifts. COMPASS paves the way for practical, metric-based uncertainty quantification for medical image segmentation.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何为医学图像分割模型的下游指标（如器官大小）提供高效且可靠的不确定性量化。  

- 动机是什么？  
  - 临床决策依赖于分割结果的衍生指标（如病灶面积），而非像素级精度；  
  - 传统方法对整个分割-指标流程视为“黑箱”，导致不确定性区间过宽，实用性差。  

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出COMPASS框架，将校准过程从输出空间转移到模型中间特征空间；  
  - 通过在低维敏感子空间中扰动中间特征，高效探索对目标指标影响最大的变化方向；  
  - 利用模型内部特征学习重要性权重，提升在分布偏移下的鲁棒性。  

- 结论是什么？  
  - 在4个医学分割任务中，COMPASS生成的置信区间显著更紧（更精确）；  
  - 在数据分布变化时仍能保持准确的覆盖率；  
  - 适用于器官面积、病灶大小等关键临床指标的不确定性评估。  

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将 conformal prediction 与模型内部特征的敏感性分析结合，实现高效校准；  
  - 利用低维敏感子空间实现“精准扰动”，避免盲目搜索，提升效率；  
  - 通过学习特征重要性权重，增强对真实世界数据分布变化的适应能力。

在医学影像分析中，医生常依赖分割结果来估算器官大小或病灶面积，这些“下游指标”比像素级精度更关键。然而，现有方法对这些指标的不确定性评估往往不准确，导致临床决策风险增加。传统方法将整个分割过程视为“黑箱”，直接对最终指标做不确定性分析，效率低且区间太宽，实用性差。

为此，研究提出COMPASS框架，核心思想是：不直接扰动输出结果，而是深入模型内部，在中间特征空间中寻找对目标指标最敏感的方向进行扰动。这就像在模型“大脑”里找到最关键的神经通路，只在这些关键路径上微调，就能高效探索指标的变化范围，从而生成更精确的不确定性区间。

title: Complexity Analysis of Normalizing Constant Estimation: from Jarzynski Equality to Annealed Importance Sampling and beyond
abstract: Given an unnormalized probability density $\pi\propto\mathrm{e}^{-V}$, estimating its normalizing constant $Z=\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}\mathrm{e}^{-V(x)}\mathrm{d}x$ or free energy $F=-\log Z$ is a crucial problem in Bayesian statistics, statistical mechanics, and machine learning. It is challenging especially in high dimensions or when $\pi$ is multimodal. To mitigate the high variance of conventional importance sampling estimators, annealing-based methods such as Jarzynski equality and annealed importance sampling are commonly adopted, yet their quantitative complexity guarantees remain largely unexplored. We take a first step toward a non-asymptotic analysis of annealed importance sampling. In particular, we derive an oracle complexity of $\widetilde{O}\left(\frac{d\beta^2{\mathcal{A}}^2}{\varepsilon^4}\right)$ for estimating $Z$ within $\varepsilon$ relative error with high probability, where $\beta$ is the smoothness of $V$ and $\mathcal{A}$ denotes the action of a curve of probability measures interpolating $\pi$ and a tractable reference distribution. Our analysis, leveraging Girsanov theorem and optimal transport, does not explicitly require isoperimetric assumptions on the target distribution. Finally, to tackle the large action of the widely used geometric interpolation, we propose a new algorithm based on reverse diffusion samplers, establish a framework for analyzing its complexity, and empirically demonstrate its efficiency in tackling multimodality.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何高效准确地估算复杂概率分布的归一化常数（如自由能）？
- 动机：传统方法在高维或多重峰分布下误差大，需更稳健的算法。
- 方法：基于热化路径的采样方法（如JEA/AIS），结合随机微分方程与最优传输理论，分析其复杂度；提出新算法（反向扩散）降低路径“作用量”。
- 结论：新方法在多重峰场景下更高效，理论复杂度优于传统路径；实验验证其在复杂分布上的优越性。
- 创新点：首次对AIS进行非渐近复杂度分析；提出基于反向扩散的新算法，降低路径作用量。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在高维或多重峰分布下，高效且准确地估算概率分布的归一化常数（即自由能）。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 传统重要性采样方法在高维或多重峰场景下方差极大，导致估计不准确；  
- 虽然热化方法（如AIS）能缓解问题，但缺乏理论上的复杂度保障，难以评估其实际效率。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 首次对AIS进行非渐近复杂度分析，结合Girsanov定理与最优传输理论；  
- 引入“路径作用量”（action）作为衡量热化路径质量的核心指标；  
- 提出基于反向扩散采样器的新算法，降低路径作用量，尤其适用于多重峰分布。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 理论上，新方法的复杂度为 $\widetilde{O}\left(\frac{d\beta^2\mathcal{A}^2}{\varepsilon^4}\right)$，优于传统几何路径；  
- 实验表明，在多重峰分布中，新算法显著提升估计效率与稳定性；  
- 适用于高维、复杂结构的概率分布建模任务。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次建立AIS的非渐近复杂度理论框架，填补理论空白；  
- 提出反向扩散路径构造方法，有效降低路径作用量，突破几何路径的性能瓶颈。

title: Concept-based Adversarial Attack: a Probabilistic Perspective
abstract: We propose a concept-based adversarial attack framework that extends beyond single-image perturbations by adopting a probabilistic perspective. Rather than modifying a single image, our method operates on an entire concept - represented by a distribution - to generate diverse adversarial examples. Preserving the concept is essential, as it ensures that the resulting adversarial images remain identifiable as instances of the original underlying category or identity. By sampling from this concept-based adversarial distribution, we generate images that maintain the original concept but vary in pose, viewpoint, or background, thereby misleading the classifier. Mathematically, this framework remains consistent with traditional adversarial attacks in a principled manner. Our theoretical and empirical results demonstrate that concept-based adversarial attacks yield more diverse adversarial examples and effectively preserve the underlying concept, while achieving higher attack efficiency.
explain_zh:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 提出一种基于“概念”的对抗攻击方法，能生成多样且保持原始概念一致的对抗样本。  

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 传统对抗攻击只修改单张图像，生成的对抗样本缺乏多样性，且可能破坏原始概念（如身份、类别）；  
  - 现实场景中，模型应能抵御对“概念”层面的攻击，而非仅针对特定图像。  

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 将一个概念（如“人脸”）建模为一个概率分布，而非单一图像；  
  - 在该分布上进行对抗扰动，生成“概念级”的对抗样本；  
  - 通过采样该分布，可得到不同姿态、背景等变化下的对抗图像，同时保持概念不变。  

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 生成的对抗样本更具多样性，能有效误导分类器；  
  - 保持原始概念（如人脸身份）不变，更贴近真实攻击场景；  
  - 攻击效率更高，适用于图像分类、人脸识别等任务。  

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次从概率视角将对抗攻击从“图像级”提升到“概念级”；  
  - 通过分布建模实现概念保持与多样性生成的统一；  
  - 理论上与传统攻击一致，兼具可解释性与实用性。

title: ConfHit: Conformal Generative Design via Nested Testing
abstract: The success of deep generative models in scientific discovery requires not only the ability to generate novel candidates but also reliable guarantees that these candidates indeed satisfy desired properties.  Recent conformal-prediction methods offer a path to such guarantees, but its application to generative modeling in drug discovery is limited by budget constraints, lack of oracle access, and distribution shift. To this end, we introduce ConfHit, a distribution-free framework that provides validity guarantees under these conditions. ConfHit formalizes two central questions: (i) Certification: whether a generated batch can be guaranteed to contain at least one hit with a user-specified confidence level, and (ii) Design: whether the generation can be refined to a compact set without weakening this guarantee. ConfHit leverages weighted exchangeability between historical and generated samples to eliminate the need for an experimental oracle, constructs multiple-sample density-ratio weighted conformal p-value to quantify statistical confidence in hits, and proposes a nested testing procedure to certify and refine candidate sets of multiple generated samples while maintaining statistical guarantees. Across representative generative molecule design tasks and a broad range of methods, ConfHit consistently delivers valid coverage guarantees at multiple confidence levels while maintaining compact certified sets, establishing a principled and reliable framework for generative modeling.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何在药物分子生成中，既生成新分子，又能可靠保证其中至少有一个“有效分子”（hit），且不依赖昂贵实验验证。
- 动机：现有方法依赖真实实验反馈（oracle），但实验成本高、数据少，且生成数据分布与历史数据不同，导致传统方法失效。
- 方法：提出ConfHit框架，核心是“加权可交换性”和“嵌套检验”：1）用历史数据与生成数据的权重关系，避免依赖真实实验；2）通过多样本密度比加权p值，量化生成分子中存在有效分子的置信度；3）用嵌套测试逐步缩小候选集，同时保持统计保证。
- 结论：在多种分子生成任务中，ConfHit在不同置信水平下均实现有效覆盖率，且生成的“保证有效”的分子集更小、更实用。
- 创新点：首次将“嵌套检验”引入生成模型，实现“生成+验证+压缩”一体化；提出无需实验oracle的加权可交换性机制，突破传统方法对真实反馈的依赖。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在药物分子生成中，确保生成的分子中至少有一个是有效的（hit），同时不依赖昂贵的实验验证。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 传统方法依赖真实实验反馈（oracle），但实验成本高、数据稀少，且生成数据分布常与历史数据不同，导致可靠性差。  
- 需要一种无需实验验证、仍能提供统计保证的生成设计方法。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出ConfHit框架，核心为：  
  - 利用“加权可交换性”：通过历史与生成样本的权重关系，替代对真实实验结果的依赖。  
  - 构建“多样本密度比加权p值”：量化生成分子中存在有效分子的统计置信度。  
  - 采用“嵌套测试”流程：逐步缩小候选分子集，同时保持统计有效性。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在多种分子生成任务中，ConfHit在不同置信水平下均实现可靠覆盖率。  
- 生成的“保证有效”分子集更小、更紧凑，实用性更强。  
- 适用于多种生成模型，具有广泛适用性。

title: Deep Latent Variable Model based Vertical Federated Learning with Flexible Alignment and Labeling Scenarios
abstract:
Federated learning (FL) has attracted significant attention for enabling collaborative learning without exposing private data. 
  Among the primary variants of FL, vertical federated learning (VFL) addresses feature-partitioned data held by multiple institutions, each holding complementary information for the same set of users. 
  However, existing VFL methods often impose restrictive assumptions such as a small number of participating parties, fully aligned data, or only using labeled data. 
  In this work, we reinterpret alignment gaps in VFL as missing data problems and propose a unified framework that accommodates both training and inference under arbitrary alignment and labeling scenarios, while supporting diverse missingness mechanisms. 
  In the experiments on 168 configurations spanning four benchmark datasets, six training-time missingness patterns, and seven testing-time missingness patterns, our method outperforms all baselines in 160 cases with an average gap of 9.6 percentage points over the next-best competitors.
  To the best of our knowledge, this is the first VFL framework to jointly handle arbitrary data alignment, unlabeled data, and multi-party collaboration all at once.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 实现一种能适应任意数据对齐和标签情况的垂直联邦学习框架。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有垂直联邦学习方法受限于严格假设（如数据完全对齐、仅使用有标签数据、参与方数量少），难以应对真实场景中常见的数据缺失与不对齐问题。  
  - 真实场景中，不同机构的数据往往存在用户或特征不一致、部分数据无标签，传统方法无法灵活处理。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 将数据对齐问题建模为“缺失数据”问题，利用深度隐变量模型（Deep Latent Variable Model）来推断缺失的特征或用户信息。  
  - 通过隐变量学习，统一处理训练和推理阶段的任意对齐缺失（如用户重叠少、特征不匹配）和标签缺失（如部分数据无标签）。  
  - 支持多种缺失机制（如随机缺失、非随机缺失），并设计联合优化策略以提升模型鲁棒性。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 在168种配置下实验，优于所有基线方法160次，平均性能高出9.6个百分点。  
  - 首个同时支持多参与方、任意对齐、无标签数据的垂直联邦学习框架，适用性强，适用于真实复杂场景。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将垂直联邦学习中的对齐问题统一建模为“缺失数据”问题，提出基于隐变量模型的统一解决方案。  
  - 突破传统方法对数据对齐和标签完整性的强依赖，实现灵活、通用的联邦协作。

title: Eliminating Inductive Bias in Reward Models with Information-Theoretic Guidance
abstract: Reward models (RMs) are crucial in reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) to align large language models (LLMs) with human values. However, RM training data is commonly recognized as low-quality, always containing preference conflicts and inductive biases, such as response length or speaking style, which can easily lead to reward overfitting and hacking. A few recent RM debiasing methods either target merely a single specific type of preference bias or only address simple linear bias relations such as Pearson coefficients. To mitigate more complicated inductive bias of reward modeling, inspired by the information bottleneck, we introduce a novel information-theoretic debiasing method called **D**ebiasing via **I**nformation optimization for **R**M (DIR). More specifically, our method trains RMs by maximizing the mutual information (MI) between preference prediction and input response pairs, while minimizing the MI between RM outputs and biased attributes of preference inputs. With the theoretical justification of information theory, DIR can handle different types of bias with more comprehensive non-linear correlations, enlarging its real-world application scenarios. In experiments, we verify the effectiveness of DIR with three types of inductive biases: response length, sycophancy, and format. Based on the numerical results, we discover that DIR can not only effectively diminish target inductive biases but also improve RLHF performances on various benchmarks with better generalization abilities.
explain_zh:
- 问题：现有奖励模型（RMs）在训练时容易受到输入数据中的诱导偏差（如回复长度、迎合倾向等）影响，导致奖励预测不准确，甚至被“欺骗”。
- 动机：为了提升奖励模型的公平性和可靠性，使其更真实地反映人类偏好，而非被表面特征误导。
- 方法：提出DIR方法，通过信息论框架优化奖励模型训练：最大化奖励预测与输入响应之间的互信息，同时最小化奖励输出与潜在偏差特征（如长度、风格）之间的互信息。
- 结论：DIR能有效消除多种复杂偏差（长度、迎合、格式），提升奖励模型的泛化能力，并在RLHF任务中取得更好性能。
- 创新点：首次将信息瓶颈理论系统应用于奖励模型去偏，能处理非线性、多类型复杂偏差，具有更强的通用性和理论保障。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 奖励模型（RMs）在训练时容易被输入数据中的诱导偏差（如回复长短、迎合语气）误导，导致奖励预测不准确甚至被“欺骗”。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有RMs常因低质量数据中的偏好冲突和偏差，产生不公正或不可靠的奖励信号，影响大模型对齐人类价值观的效果。  
- 需要一种能系统性消除多种复杂偏差的方法，提升奖励模型的真实性和泛化能力。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出DIR方法，基于信息论框架：  
  - 最大化奖励预测与输入响应之间的互信息（保留有用偏好信息）；  
  - 最小化奖励输出与潜在偏差特征（如长度、风格）之间的互信息（去除无关干扰）；  
  - 通过联合优化实现“只学人类偏好，不学表面特征”。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在三种典型偏差（回复长度、迎合倾向、格式偏好）上均显著降低偏差影响；  
- 提升RLHF任务中的模型性能与泛化能力，在多个基准测试中表现更优。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将信息瓶颈理论系统引入RMs去偏，实现对非线性、多类型偏差的统一建模；  
- 具有坚实的理论基础，适用范围广，超越传统仅处理线性关系

title: Flow Map Learning via Games
abstract: Diffusion and flow-based models benefit from simple regression losses, but inference (i.e, producing samples) incurs significant computational overhead because it requires integration. Consistency models address this overhead by directly learning the flow maps along the ODE trajectory, revealing a design space for the learning problem between one-step and many-step approaches. However, existing consistency training methods feature computational challenges such as requiring model inverses or backpropagation through iterated model calls, and do not always prove that the desired ODE flow map is a solution to the loss. We introduce CurlFlow, an approach for learning flow maps that bypasses explicit invertibility constraints and expensive differentiation through model iteration. CurlFlow trains a model to compute both the ODE solutions and the implied velocity from scratch by following non-conservative dynamics (i.e., those with curl) with stationary point at the desired flow map. On the CIFAR image benchmark, CurlFlow attains a favorable relationship of FID to step count, relative to flow matching, MeanFlow, and several other flow map learning methods.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何高效且准确地学习生成模型中的流映射，以减少采样时的计算开销。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有流模型（如扩散模型）在采样时需多次积分，计算成本高。
  - 一致性模型虽能减少步骤，但存在反向传播复杂、需模型可逆等限制。
  - 需要一种更简单、稳定且无需显式可逆性的流映射学习方法。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 引入“非保守动力学”（含旋度的动态），让模型在训练中直接学习流映射和对应速度。
  - 通过设计一个在目标流映射处有稳定不动点的动态系统，使模型收敛到期望解。
  - 不依赖模型逆或迭代反向传播，避免了计算瓶颈。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在CIFAR-10上，CurlFlow在FID与采样步数之间取得良好平衡。
  - 相比流匹配、MeanFlow等方法，性能更优或相当，且更高效。
  - 适用于图像生成等需要快速采样的生成建模任务。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将“非保守动力学”引入流映射学习，突破传统保守性假设。
  - 无需模型可逆性或反向传播迭代，简化训练与推理。
  - 提供理论保证：目标流映射是优化目标的稳定解。

title: From Predictors to Samplers via the Training Trajectory
abstract: Sampling from trained predictors is fundamental for interpretability and as a compute-light alternative to diffusion models, but local samplers struggle on the rugged, high-frequency functions such models learn. We observe that standard neural‑network training implicitly produces a coarse‑to‑fine sequence of models. Early checkpoints suppress high‑degree/ high‑frequency components (Boolean monomials; spherical harmonics under NTK), while later checkpoints restore detail. We exploit this by running a simple annealed sampler across the training trajectory, using early checkpoints for high‑mobility proposals and later ones for refinement. In the Boolean domain, this can turn the exponential bottleneck arising from rugged landscapes or needle gadgets into a near-linear one. In the continuous domain, under the NTK regime, this corresponds to smoothing under the NTK kernel. Requiring no additional compute, our method shows strong empirical gains across a variety of synthetic and real-world tasks, including  constrained sampling tasks that diffusion models are unable to natively handle.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何高效、准确地从训练好的神经网络预测器中采样，尤其是在函数表面崎岖、高频的复杂情况下。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统局部采样方法在高频率、崎岖的函数上效率极低，容易陷入局部最优。
  - 现有方法（如扩散模型）计算成本高，难以在资源受限场景使用。
  - 训练过程本身隐含了从粗到细的模型演化路径，可被利用来提升采样效率。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 利用训练过程中的多个中间模型（checkpoint），构建“粗到细”的采样路径。
  - 早期模型（低频、平滑）用于快速探索空间（高移动性）。
  - 后期模型（高频、细节丰富）用于精细调整和收敛。
  - 采用退火策略，逐步从早期模型过渡到后期模型进行采样。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在布尔域中，将原本指数级的采样瓶颈降低至近线性。
  - 在连续域中，等价于在NTK核下平滑，提升采样稳定性。
  - 无需额外计算，适用于合成与真实任务，包括扩散模型难以处理的约束采样。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将训练轨迹本身作为采样资源，而非仅关注最终模型。
  - 提出“粗到细”的采样框架，利用训练过程中的隐式演化规律。
  - 无需额外训练或模型修改，实现高效采样，兼具理论解释与实证优势。

title: From Samples to Scenarios: A New Paradigm for Probabilistic Forecasting
abstract: Most state-of-the-art probabilistic time series forecasting models rely on sampling to represent future uncertainty. However, this paradigm suffers from inherent limitations, such as lacking explicit probabilities, inadequate coverage, and high computational costs. In this work, we introduce **Probabilistic Scenarios**, an alternative paradigm designed to address the limitations of sampling. It operates by directly producing a finite set of {Scenario, Probability} pairs, thus avoiding Monte Carlo-like approximation. To validate this paradigm, we propose **TimePrism**, a simple model composed of only three parallel linear layers. Surprisingly, TimePrism achieves 9 out of 10 state-of-the-art results across five benchmark datasets on two metrics. The effectiveness of our paradigm comes from a fundamental reframing of the learning objective. Instead of modeling an entire continuous probability space, the model learns to represent a set of plausible scenarios and corresponding probabilities. Our work demonstrates the potential of the Probabilistic Scenarios paradigm, opening a promising research direction in forecasting beyond sampling.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 传统基于采样的概率时间序列预测方法存在概率表示不明确、覆盖不全和计算成本高等问题。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 采样方法难以精确表达未来不确定性，且效率低，限制了实际应用。
  - 需要一种更高效、更可解释的替代方案来建模未来可能的多种发展路径。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出“概率场景”新范式：直接输出有限的{场景, 概率}对，而非通过采样逼近分布。
  - 设计TimePrism模型：仅由三个并行线性层构成，结构极简。
  - 训练目标为学习每个场景的合理性与对应概率，避免蒙特卡洛近似。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在5个基准数据集上，TimePrism在10项指标中有9项达到SOTA。
  - 模型轻量高效，推理速度快，适合资源受限场景。
  - 适用于需要清晰、可解释未来路径的预测任务。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次提出“概率场景”范式，从“采样逼近分布”转向“直接生成场景+概率”。
  - 极简模型TimePrism证明简单结构也能实现顶尖性能，挑战复杂模型必要性。
  - 重新定义学习目标：聚焦于可解释的离散场景而非连续概率空间。

title: Laplacian Kernelized Bandit
abstract: We study multi-user contextual bandits where users are related by a graph and their reward functions exhibit both non-linear behavior and graph homophily. We introduce a principled joint penalty for the collection of user reward functions $\\{f_u\\}$, combining a graph smoothness term based on RKHS distances with an individual roughness penalty. Our central contribution is proving that this penalty is equivalent to the squared norm within a single, unified _multi-user RKHS_. We explicitly derive its reproducing kernel, which elegantly fuses the graph Laplacian with the base arm kernel. This unification allows us to reframe the problem as learning a single "lifted" function, enabling the design of principled algorithms, LK-GP-UCB and LK-GP-TS, that leverage Gaussian Process posteriors over this new kernel for exploration. We provide high-probability regret bounds that scale with an _effective dimension_ of the multi-user kernel, replacing dependencies on user count or ambient dimension. Empirically, our methods outperform strong linear and non-graph-aware baselines in non-linear settings and remain competitive even when the true rewards are linear. Our work delivers a unified, theoretically grounded, and practical framework that bridges Laplacian regularization with kernelized bandits for structured exploration.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 在存在用户间图结构关系的多用户上下文Bandit问题中，如何有效建模非线性奖励函数并利用用户间的同质性进行高效探索。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现实场景中用户行为常具有图结构关联（如社交网络），且奖励函数常为非线性，但现有方法难以同时建模图结构与非线性，导致探索效率低。
  - 传统方法要么忽略图结构，要么仅处理线性奖励，无法兼顾结构信息与复杂函数拟合能力。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出一种联合惩罚项：结合图平滑性（基于RKHS距离）与个体函数粗糙度惩罚，统一为一个“多用户RKHS”中的函数范数。
  - 显式推导出该空间的再生核，巧妙融合图拉普拉斯与基础臂核，形成“拉普拉斯核化”新核函数。
  - 将原问题转化为学习一个“升维”后的单一函数，使用高斯过程（GP）进行后验推断，设计LK-GP-UCB与LK-GP-TS算法进行探索。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 理论上：提供高概率后悔界，依赖于新核的有效维度，不随用户数或特征维度增长。
  - 实验上：在非线性场景下显著优于线性与非图感知基线；即使奖励为线性，也保持竞争力。
  - 适用范围：适用于具有图结构关系的多用户推荐、个性化服务等非线性上下文Bandit场景。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将图拉普拉斯正则与核方法在RKHS框架下统一，构建出可显式计算的“拉普拉斯核化”核函数。
  - 将多用户问题转化为单函数学习，实现结构化探索的理论与算法统一。
  - 提供可扩展的后悔界，摆脱对用户数量或输入维度的依赖，更具实用性。

title: Latent Veracity Inference for Identifying Errors in Stepwise Reasoning
abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning has advanced the capabilities and transparency of language models (LMs); however, reasoning chains can contain inaccurate statements that reduce performance and trustworthiness. To address this, we propose to augment each reasoning step in a CoT with a latent veracity (or correctness) variable. To efficiently explore this expanded space, we introduce Veracity Search (VS), a discrete search algorithm over veracity assignments. It performs otherwise intractable inference in the posterior distribution over latent veracity values by leveraging the LM's joint likelihood over veracity and the final answer as a proxy reward. This efficient inference-time verification method facilitates supervised fine-tuning of an Amortized Veracity Inference (AVI) machine by providing pseudo-labels for veracity. AVI generalizes VS, enabling accurate zero-shot veracity inference in novel contexts. Empirical results demonstrate that VS reliably identifies errors in logical (ProntoQA), mathematical (GSM8K), and commonsense (CommonsenseQA) reasoning benchmarks, with AVI achieving comparable zero-shot accuracy. Finally, we demonstrate the utility of latent veracity inference for providing feedback during self-correction and self-improvement.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 识别语言模型在逐步推理过程中产生的错误陈述。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - CoT推理虽提升透明度，但其中可能包含错误，影响模型性能与可信度。
  - 现有方法难以高效验证每一步推理的正确性，尤其在推理时。
  - 需要一种可扩展、无需额外标注的错误检测机制。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 为每个推理步骤引入一个“潜在可信度”变量，表示该步是否正确。
  - 提出Veracity Search（VS）：一种基于语言模型联合似然的离散搜索算法，高效推断可信度分布。
  - 用VS生成伪标签，训练一个可泛化的Amortized Veracity Inference（AVI）模型。
  - AVI可在新场景中实现零样本可信度判断。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - VS在逻辑、数学、常识推理任务中均能有效识别错误。
  - AVI在零样本下达到与VS相当的准确率。
  - 可用于自我纠错与模型自提升，提升推理可靠性。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将“潜在可信度”建模为可推断的变量，实现推理过程的可验证性。
  - 提出VS算法，以语言模型自身能力作为奖励信号，实现高效推理时验证。
  - 构建AVI模型，实现从“一次性验证”到“通用可信度判断”的跨越。

title: Learning Distributions over Permutations and Rankings with Factorized Representations
abstract: Learning distributions over permutations is a fundamental problem in machine learning, with applications in ranking, combinatorial optimization, structured prediction, and data association. Existing methods rely on mixtures of parametric families or neural networks with expensive variational inference procedures. In this work, we propose a novel approach that leverages alternative representations for permutations, including Lehmer codes, Fisher-Yates draws, and Insertion-Vectors. These representations form a bijection with the symmetric group, allowing for unconstrained learning using conventional deep learning techniques, and can represent any probability distribution over permutations. Our approach enables a trade-off between expressivity of the model family and computational requirements. In the least expressive and most computationally efficient case our method subsumes previous families of well established probabilistic models over permutations, including Mallow's and the Repeated Insertion Model. Experiments indicate our method significantly outperforms current approaches on the jigsaw puzzle benchmark, a common task for permutation learning. However, we argue this benchmark is limited in its ability to assess learning probability distributions, as the target is a delta distribution (i.e., a single correct solution exists). We therefore propose two additional benchmarks: learning cyclic permutations and re-ranking movies based on user preference. We show that our method learns non-trivial distributions even in the least expressive mode, while traditional models fail to even generate valid permutations in this setting.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 学习排列（permutations）上的概率分布，用于排名、组合优化等任务。  

- 动机是什么？  
  - 传统方法依赖复杂的变分推断或混合模型，计算成本高且表达能力有限。  
  - 现有方法难以有效建模非平凡的排列分布，尤其在简单模型下表现差。  

- 方法是什么？  
  - 将排列映射为三种可学习的“编码”：Lehmer码、Fisher-Yates抽样序列、插入向量（Insertion-Vectors）。  
  - 这些编码与排列一一对应（双射），可转化为实数空间上的连续表示。  
  - 使用标准深度学习模型（如MLP）在编码空间中直接建模概率分布，避免复杂推断。  

- 结论是什么？  
  - 在拼图任务上显著优于现有方法，尤其在低表达力模式下仍能学习有效分布。  
  - 提出的新基准（循环排列、电影重排序）揭示传统模型在非单点分布任务中失效。  
  - 该方法在表达力与效率间可灵活权衡，适用范围广。  

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将排列的数学编码（如Lehmer码）用于深度学习建模，实现无约束训练。  
  - 通过编码空间建模，统一了经典概率模型（如Mallows、重复插入模型）为特例。  
  - 提出更合理的评估基准，推动对排列分布学习的深入研究。

title: On the Mechanisms of Collaborative Learning in VAE Recommenders
abstract:
Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) are a powerful alternative to matrix factorization for recommendation. A common technique in VAE-based collaborative filtering (CF) consists in applying binary input masking to user interaction vectors, which improves performance but remains underexplored theoretically. In this work, we analyze how collaboration arises in VAE-based CF and show it is governed by \emph{latent proximity}: we derive a latent sharing radius that informs when an SGD update on one user strictly reduces the loss on another user, with influence decaying as the latent Wasserstein distance increases. We further study the induced geometry: with clean inputs, VAE‑based CF primarily exploits \emph{local} collaboration between input‑similar users and under‑utilizes \emph{global} collaboration between far‑but‑related users. We compare two mechanisms that encourage \emph{global} mixing and characterize their trade‑offs: \ding{172} $\beta$‑KL regularization directly tightens the information bottleneck, promoting posterior overlap but risking representational collapse if too large; \ding{173} input masking induces stochastic \emph{geometric} contractions and expansions, which can bring distant users onto the same latent neighborhood but also introduce neighborhood drift.
To preserve user identity while enabling global consistency, we propose an anchor regularizer that aligns user posteriors with item embeddings, stabilizing users under masking and facilitating signal sharing across related items. Our analyses are validated on the Netflix, MovieLens-20M, and Million Song datasets. We also successfully deployed our proposed algorithm on an Amazon streaming platform following a successful online experiment.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 揭示了基于变分自编码器（VAE）的推荐系统中“协作学习”的内在机制。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - VAE推荐系统中通过输入掩码提升性能，但其背后的协作机制尚不清晰。
  - 现有方法在用户间协作上存在局限：过度依赖相似用户之间的局部协作，忽视远距离但相关用户的全局协作。
  - 需要理解并改进这种协作机制，以提升推荐效果和稳定性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出“潜在距离”（latent proximity）概念，用Wasserstein距离衡量用户间协作潜力。
  - 分析两种促进全局协作的机制：① β-KL正则化（收紧信息瓶颈）；② 输入掩码（引入随机几何收缩与扩张）。
  - 设计“锚点正则化”模块，将用户后验分布与物品嵌入对齐，防止身份混淆，稳定协作过程。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 理论与实验证明：协作强度随用户潜在距离增加而衰减。
  - 输入掩码能有效促进远距离用户协作，但可能导致“邻居漂移”。
  - 锚点正则化显著提升稳定性，且在Netflix、MovieLens、Million Song及亚马逊流媒体平台均验证有效。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次从潜在空间几何角度解释VAE推荐中的协作机制。
  - 提出“潜在共享半径”理论，量化协作影响范围。
  - 设计锚点正则化，平衡用户身份保持与跨用户信号共享，兼具理论与实用价值。

title: Optimizing Data Augmentation through Bayesian Model Selection
abstract:
Data Augmentation (DA) has become an essential tool to improve robustness and generalization of modern machine learning. 
However, when deciding on DA strategies it is critical to choose parameters carefully, and this can be a daunting task which is traditionally left to trial-and-error or expensive optimization based on validation performance.
In this paper, we counter these limitations by proposing a novel framework for optimizing DA. 
In particular, we take a probabilistic view of DA, which leads to the interpretation of augmentation parameters as model (hyper)-parameters, and the optimization of the marginal likelihood with respect to these parameters as a Bayesian model selection problem. 
Due to its intractability, we derive a tractable ELBO, which allows us to optimize augmentation parameters jointly with model parameters. We provide extensive theoretical results on variational approximation quality, generalization guarantees, invariance properties, and connections to empirical Bayes.
Through experiments on computer vision and NLP tasks, we show that our approach improves calibration and yields robust performance over fixed or no augmentation. 
Our work provides a rigorous foundation for optimizing DA through Bayesian principles with significant potential for robust machine learning.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何自动、高效地选择最优的数据增强策略（参数）？

- 方法：将数据增强参数视为“超参数”，用贝叶斯框架建模，通过最大化边缘似然（即变分下界ELBO）联合优化模型和增强参数。
- 结论：在图像和文本任务中，该方法显著提升模型校准性与鲁棒性，优于固定或无增强策略，且理论基础扎实。

- 整个过程端到端可训练，实现“模型+增强策略”同步优化。

title: Reliable Probabilistic Forecasting of Irregular Time Series through Marginalization-Consistent Flows
abstract:
Probabilistic forecasting of joint distributions for irregular time series with missing values is an underexplored area in machine learning. Existing models, such as Gaussian Process Regression and ProFITi, are limited: while ProFITi is highly expressive due to its use of normalizing flows, it often produces unrealistic predictions because it lacks marginalization consistency—marginal distributions of subsets of variables may not match those predicted directly, leading to inaccurate marginal forecasts when trained on joints.
We propose MOSES (Mixtures of Separable Flows), a novel model that parametrizes a stochastic process via a mixture of normalizing flows, where each component combines a latent multivariate Gaussian with separable univariate transformations. This design allows MOSES to be analytically marginalized, enabling accurate and reliable predictions for various probabilistic queries.
Experiments on four datasets show that MOSES achieves highly accurate joint and marginal predictions. Thanks to its inherent marginalization consistency, MOSES significantly outperforms all baselines—including ProFITi—on marginal predictions.
For joint predictions, it beats all other consistent models and performs close to or slightly worse than ProFITi.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何对存在缺失值的不规则时间序列进行可靠的概率预测。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有模型（如ProFITi）虽表达能力强，但缺乏“边缘一致性”：预测的子集分布与直接预测不一致，导致边缘预测不准。
  - 实际应用中，用户常需关注特定时间点或变量的分布（如某时刻的温度概率），因此边缘预测的可靠性至关重要。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出MOSES模型：用混合的可分离归一化流（Mixture of Separable Flows）建模随机过程。
  - 每个流组件由一个高斯潜变量 + 可分离的单变量变换构成，保证边缘分布可解析计算。
  - 通过联合训练，模型能同时学习完整联合分布和任意子集的边缘分布。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在4个数据集上，MOSES在边缘预测上显著优于所有基线（包括ProFITi）。
  - 联合预测性能接近甚至略逊于ProFITi，但远超其他一致性模型。
  - 适用于需要高可靠性边缘预测的不规则时间序列场景（如医疗监测、金融波动）。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次在归一化流框架中实现“边缘一致性”：通过可分离流+混合结构，使边缘分布可解析计算。
  - 在保持强表达能力的同时，解决了传统流模型边缘不一致的致命缺陷。

title: SNAP-UQ: Self-supervised Next-Activation Prediction for Single-Pass Uncertainty in TinyML
abstract: Reliable uncertainty estimation is a key missing piece for on-device monitoring in TinyML: microcontrollers must detect failures, distribution shift, or accuracy drops under strict flash/latency budgets, yet common uncertainty approaches (deep ensembles, MC dropout, early exits, temporal buffering) typically require multiple passes, extra branches, or state that is impractical on milliwatt hardware. This paper proposes a novel and practical method, SNAP-UQ, for single-pass, label-free uncertainty estimation based on depth-wise next-activation prediction. SNAP-UQ taps a small set of backbone layers and uses tiny int8 heads to predict the mean and scale of the next activation from a low-rank projection of the previous one; the resulting standardized prediction error forms a depth-wise surprisal signal that is aggregated and mapped through a lightweight monotone calibrator into an actionable uncertainty score. The design introduces no temporal buffers or auxiliary exits and preserves state-free inference, while increasing deployment footprint by only a few tens of kilobytes. Across vision and audio backbones, SNAP-UQ reduces flash and latency relative to early-exit and deep-ensemble baselines (typically $\sim$40--60% smaller and $\sim$25--35% faster), with several competing methods at similar accuracy often exceeding MCU memory limits. On corrupted streams, it improves accuracy-drop event detection by multiple AUPRC points and maintains strong failure detection (AUROC $\approx 0.9$) in a single forward pass. By grounding uncertainty in layer-to-layer dynamics rather than solely in output confidence, SNAP-UQ offers a novel, resource-efficient basis for robust TinyML monitoring. Our code is available at: https://github.com/Ism-ail11/SNAP-UQ
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 在资源极度受限的微型机器学习（TinyML）设备上，实现单次推理过程中的可靠不确定性估计。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 传统不确定性方法（如集成、Dropout、早停）需多次推理或额外分支，不适用于毫瓦级微控制器。  
  - TinyML设备需实时检测故障、分布偏移或精度下降，但现有方法在内存和延迟上不可行。  
  - 缺乏无需标签、无需状态、低开销的单次推理不确定性估计方案。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出SNAP-UQ：基于深度卷积层间激活预测的自监督方法。  
  - 从主干网络中选取少量层，用极小的int8头预测下一层激活的均值与尺度。  
  - 利用前一层激活的低秩投影，计算标准化预测误差，生成“意外度”信号。  
  - 将各层意外度聚合，并通过轻量单调校准器输出最终不确定性分数。

- 结论是什么？

  - 单次前向传播即可完成，AUROC达0.9，适用于真实边缘设备部署。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将层间激活预测用于不确定性估计，基于动态行为而非输出置信度。  
  - 完全无状态、无需额外分支或缓冲，极低资源开销（仅增加几十KB）。

title: Scaling Multi-Task Bayesian Optimization with Large Language Models
abstract: In multi-task Bayesian optimization, the goal is to leverage experience from optimizing existing tasks to improve the efficiency of optimizing new ones. While approaches using multi-task Gaussian processes or deep kernel transfer exist, the performance improvement is marginal when scaling beyond a moderate number of tasks. We introduce **BOLT**, an initialization-only transfer strategy that distills prior BO runs into an LLM which proposes candidates for new tasks, while the surrogate at test time remains single-task. The LLM is periodically fine-tuned on top solutions from completed runs, creating a closed loop where better BO outputs yield better initializations over time. This decoupled design scales to roughly 1500 tasks without the saturation observed for shared-surrogate MTBO and adds only a small, amortized overhead relative to the BO inner loops. We evaluate on two domains: database query optimization and antimicrobial peptide design. We demonstrate that LLM-generated initializations steadily improve and accelerate BO, and with sufficient fine-tuning, a few LLM samples often match or surpass full ''from-scratch'' BO with far fewer oracle calls.

title: Singleton-Optimized Conformal Prediction
abstract: Conformal prediction can be used to construct prediction sets that cover the true outcome with a desired probability, but can sometimes lead to large prediction sets that are costly in practice. The most useful outcome is a singleton prediction---an unambiguous decision---yet existing efficiency-oriented methods primarily optimize average set size. Motivated by this, we propose a new non-conformity score that is motivated by minimizing the probability of producing non-singleton sets while maintaining coverage. Starting from a non-convex constrained optimization problem as a motivation, we provide a convex-geometric reformulation and associated algorithm for computing the non-conformity score and associated split conformal prediction sets in $O(K)$ time for $K$-class problems. Using this score in split conformal prediction, we introduce Singleton-Optimized Conformal Prediction (SOCOP). We evaluate our method in experiments on image classification and LLM multiple-choice answering, comparing with standard non-conformity scores such as the (negative) label probability estimates and their cumulative distribution function; both of which are motivated by aiming to optimize average length. The results show that SOCOP increases singleton frequency (sometimes by over 20\%) compared to the above scores, with minimal impact on average set size.

title: Source-Guided Flow Matching
abstract: Guidance of generative models is typically achieved by modifying the probability flow vector field through the addition of a guidance field. In this paper, we instead propose the Source-Guided Flow Matching (SGFM) framework, which modifies the source distribution directly while keeping the pre-trained vector field intact. This reduces the guidance problem to a well-defined problem of sampling from the source distribution. We theoretically show that SGFM recovers the desired target distribution exactly. Furthermore, we provide bounds on the Wasserstein error for the generated distribution when using an approximate sampler of the source distribution and an approximate vector field. The key benefit of our approach is that it allows the user to flexibly choose the sampling method depending on their specific problem. To illustrate this, we systematically compare different sampling methods and discuss conditions for asymptotically exact guidance. Moreover, our framework integrates well with optimal flow matching models since the straight transport map generated by the vector field is preserved. Experimental results on synthetic 2D benchmarks, physics-informed generative tasks, and imaging inverse problems demonstrate the effectiveness and flexibility of the proposed framework.

title: Stop Guessing: Choosing the Optimization-Consistent Uncertainty Measurement for Evidential Deep Learning
abstract: Evidential Deep Learning (EDL) has emerged as a promising framework for uncertainty estimation in classification tasks by modeling predictive uncertainty with a Dirichlet prior. Despite its empirical success, prior work has primarily focused on the probabilistic properties of the Dirichlet distribution, leaving the role of optimization dynamics during training underexplored.  In this paper, we revisit EDL through the lens of optimization and establish a non-trivial connection: minimizing the expected cross-entropy loss over the Dirichlet prior implicitly encourages solutions akin to multi-class Support Vector Machines, maximizing decision margins. Motivated by this observation, we introduce the \emph{optimization-consistency principle}, which deems an uncertainty measure valid if its value decreases as samples approach the global optimum of the training objective. This principle provides a new criterion for evaluating and designing uncertainty measures that are consistent with the optimization dynamics. Building on this foundation, we further propose a novel measure, \emph{Margin-aware Predictive Uncertainty (MPU)}, which directly captures the separation between target and non-target evidence. Extensive experiments on out-of-distribution detection and classification-with-rejection benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our propositions.
explain_zh:
- 问题：现有证据深度学习（EDL）中，不确定性度量与优化过程不一致，导致可靠性存疑。
- 动机：当前方法多关注概率性质，忽视训练优化过程对不确定性的隐含影响，可能使不确定性估计“猜错”。
- 方法：提出“优化一致性原则”——不确定性应随样本趋近最优解而减小；基于此设计新度量MPU，直接衡量目标与非目标证据的间隔。
- 结论：MPU在分布外检测和可拒绝分类任务中表现更优，验证了优化一致性原则的有效性。
- 创新点：首次从优化角度重新审视EDL，提出“优化一致性”新准则，并设计出与优化过程一致的MPU度量。

---

- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 现有证据深度学习（EDL）中的不确定性度量，可能与模型训练的优化过程不一致，导致估计不可靠。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 多数研究只关注Dirichlet分布的数学性质，忽略了训练优化如何影响不确定性，使得不确定性“猜错”方向。  
  - 若不确定性不能随模型趋近最优而减小，其可信度存疑。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出“优化一致性原则”：一个有效的不确定性度量，必须在样本接近全局最优时单调下降。  
  - 基于此原则，设计新度量MPU，直接衡量目标类别与非目标类别的证据间隔（即决策边界距离）。  
  - 推导表明，最小化交叉熵损失隐含地推动模型向SVM式最大间隔方向优化，MPU与之天然契合。

- 结论是什么？  
  - MPU在分布外检测和可拒绝分类任务中显著优于现有方法。  
  - 实验证明优化一致性原则能有效筛选出更可靠的不确定性度量。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次从优化动态视角重新审视EDL，提出“优化一致性”作为新评价标准。  
  - 设计出与优化过程一致的MPU度量，实现不确定性估计与训练目标的内在协同。

title: AI-for-Science Low-code Platform with Bayesian Adversarial Multi-Agent Framework
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate potentials for automating scientific code generation but face challenges in reliability, error propagation in multi-agent workflows, and evaluation in domains with ill-defined success metrics. We present a Bayesian adversarial multi-agent framework specifically designed for AI for Science (AI4S) tasks in the form of a Low-code Platform (LCP). Three LLM-based agents are coordinated under the Bayesian framework: a Task Manager that structures user inputs into actionable plans and adaptive test cases, a Code Generator that produces candidate solutions, and an Evaluator providing comprehensive feedback. The framework employs an adversarial loop where the Task Manager iteratively refines test cases to challenge the Code Generator, while prompt distributions are dynamically updated using Bayesian principles by integrating code quality metrics: functional correctness, structural alignment, and static analysis. This co-optimization of tests and code reduces dependence on LLM reliability and addresses evaluation uncertainty inherent to scientific tasks. LCP also streamlines human-AI collaboration by translating non-expert prompts into domain-specific requirements, bypassing the need for manual prompt engineering by practitioners without coding backgrounds. Benchmark evaluations demonstrate LCP’s effectiveness in generating robust code while minimizing error propagation. The proposed platform is also tested on an Earth Science cross-disciplinary task and demonstrates strong reliability, outperforming competing models.

title: CONSIGN: Conformal Segmentation Informed by Spatial Groupings via Decomposition
abstract:
Most machine learning-based image segmentation models produce pixel-wise confidence scores that represent the model’s predicted probability for each class label at every pixel. While this information can be particularly valuable in high-stakes domains such as medical imaging, these scores are heuristic in nature and do not constitute rigorous quantitative uncertainty estimates.
Conformal prediction (CP) provides a principled framework for transforming heuristic confidence scores into statistically valid uncertainty estimates. However, applying CP directly to image segmentation ignores the spatial correlations between pixels, a fundamental characteristic of image data. This can result in overly conservative and less interpretable uncertainty estimates.
To address this, we propose CONSIGN (*Conformal Segmentation Informed by Spatial Groupings via Decomposition*), a CP-based method that incorporates spatial correlations to improve uncertainty quantification in image segmentation.
Our method generates meaningful prediction sets that come with user-specified, high-probability error guarantees.
It is compatible with any pre-trained segmentation model capable of generating multiple sample outputs.
We evaluate CONSIGN against two CP baselines across three medical imaging datasets and two COCO dataset subsets, using three different pre-trained segmentation models. Results demonstrate that accounting for spatial structure significantly improves performance across multiple metrics and enhances the quality of uncertainty estimates.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在图像分割任务中生成既准确又具有统计保障的不确定性估计，同时考虑像素间的空间相关性。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统分割模型输出的置信度是启发式的，缺乏严格的统计保证。
  - 直接对像素应用分层预测（CP）会忽略图像中像素的空间结构，导致不确定性估计过于保守、难以解释。
  - 在医疗影像等高风险领域，需要可靠且可解释的不确定性评估。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出CONSIGN方法，基于分解思想将图像划分为有意义的空间分组（如连通区域）。
  - 在这些空间分组上应用分层预测（CP），而非单个像素。
  - 利用预训练分割模型生成多个样本输出，用于构建校准集并计算分组级别的预测集。
  - 推理时，为每个空间分组输出一个包含真实标签的高概率预测集合。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个医学影像和COCO数据集上，CONSIGN显著优于两个CP基线方法。
  - 提升了不确定性估计的质量，预测集更紧凑、更少保守，且更具可解释性。
  - 适用于任何能输出多样本的预训练分割模型，具有良好的通用性。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将空间分组（如连通区域）引入分层预测框架，显式建模像素间的空间相关性。
  - 通过分解策略实现对图像结构的自适应建模，提升不确定性估计的合理性。
  - 方法无需重新训练模型，仅依赖已有模型输出，易于集成与部署。

title: Clustering by Denoising: Latent plug-and-play diffusion for single-cell embeddings
abstract:
Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) enables the study of cellular heterogeneity. Yet, clustering accuracy, and with it downstream analyses based on cell labels, remain challenging due to measurement noise and biological variability. In standard latent spaces (e.g., obtained through PCA), data from different cell types can be projected close together, making accurate clustering difficult.
We introduce a latent plug-and-play diffusion framework that separates the observation and denoising space. 
This separation is operationalized through a novel Gibbs sampling procedure: the learned diffusion prior is applied in a low-dimensional latent space to perform denoising, while to steer this process, noise is reintroduced into the original high-dimensional observation space. 
This unique ``input-space steering'' ensures the denoising trajectory remains faithful to the original data structure. Our approach offers three key advantages:
(1) adaptive noise handling via a tunable balance between prior and observed data; (2) uncertainty quantification through principled uncertainty estimates for downstream analysis; and (3) generalizable denoising by leveraging clean reference data to denoise noisier datasets, and via averaging, improve quality beyond the training set.
We evaluate robustness on both synthetic and real single-cell genomics data. Our method improves clustering accuracy on synthetic data across varied noise levels and dataset shifts. On real-world single-cell data,  our method demonstrates improved biological coherence in the resulting cell clusters, with cluster boundaries that better align with known cell type markers and developmental trajectories.

title: Detection of unknown unknowns in autonomous systems
abstract: Unknown unknowns (U2s) are deployment-time scenarios absent from development/testing. Unlike conventional anomalies, U2s are not out-of-distribution (OOD); they stem from changes in underlying system dynamics without a distribution shift from normal data. Thus, existing multi-variate time series anomaly detection (MTAD) methods—which rely on distribution-shift cues—are ill-suited for U2 detection. Specifically: (i) we show most anomaly datasets exhibit distribution shift between normal and anomalous data and therefore are not representative of U2s; (ii) we introduce eight U2 benchmarks where training data contain OOD anomalies but no U2s, while test sets contain both OOD anomalies and U2s; (iii) we demonstrate that state-of-the-art (SOTA) MTAD results often depend on impractical enhancements: point adjustment (PA) (uses ground truth to flip false negatives to true positives, inflating precision) and threshold learning with data leakage (TL) (tuning thresholds on test data and labels); (iv) with PA+TL, even untrained deterministic methods can match or surpass MTAD baselines; (v) without PA/TL, existing MTAD methods degrade sharply on U2 benchmarks. Finally, we present sparse model identification–enhanced anomaly detection (SPIE-AD), a model-recovery-and-conformance, zero-shot MTAD approach that outperforms baselines on all eight U2 benchmarks and on six additional real-world MTAD datasets—without PA or TL.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 检测自动驾驶系统在实际运行中遇到的“未知的未知”（U2）场景，即开发测试阶段未覆盖、且不表现为数据分布异常的新情况。  

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有异常检测方法依赖数据分布偏移（OOD）信号，但U2并非分布变化，而是系统动态本质改变，导致现有方法失效。  
  - 多数公开异常数据集本身存在分布偏移，无法真实反映U2场景，误导研究方向。  

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出八组新的U2基准测试集，区分OOD异常与U2，确保训练数据无U2，测试数据包含两者。  
  - 设计SPIE-AD方法：基于稀疏模型识别与模型一致性验证的零样本检测框架，无需训练或调参。  
  - 检测流程：通过构建系统动态的稀疏模型，判断实时数据是否符合预期行为模式，不依赖分布假设。  

- 结论是什么？  
  - SOTA MTAD方法在U2场景下性能大幅下降，仅因PA+TL等不现实增强才表现良好。  
  - SPIE-AD在八组U2基准和六个真实数据集上均优于现有方法，且无需PA/TL，具备强泛化能力。  

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次系统定义并构建U2基准，揭示现有方法的局限性。  
  - 提出零样本、无需分布假设的SPIE-AD框架，直接面向真实U2检测需求。

title: Don't Forget Its Variance! The Minimum Path Variance Principle for Accurate and Stable Score-Based Models
abstract:
Score-based methods are powerful  across machine learning, but they face a paradox: theoretically path-independent, yet practically path-dependent.
	We resolve this by proving that practical training objectives differ from the ideal, ground-truth objective by a crucial, overlooked term: the path variance of the score function.
	We propose the MinPV (**Min**imum **P**ath **V**ariance) Principle to minimize this path variance. 
	Our key contribution is deriving a closed-form expression for the variance, making optimization tractable. 
	By parameterizing the path with a flexible Kumaraswamy Mixture Model, our method learns data-adaptive, low-variance paths without heuristic manual selection. 
	This principled optimization of the complete objective yields more accurate and stable estimators, establishing new state-of-the-art results on challenging benchmarks and providing a general framework for optimizing score-based interpolation.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 传统基于得分的模型在实际训练中存在路径依赖问题，导致性能不稳定和不准确。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 理论上得分模型应与路径无关，但实际训练中路径选择显著影响结果，现有方法依赖人工经验选择路径，缺乏理论指导。
  - 忽略路径方差会引入偏差，导致模型性能下降。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出最小路径方差（MinPV）原则，通过最小化得分函数的路径方差来优化模型。
  - 推导出路径方差的闭式表达式，使优化可行。
  - 使用灵活的Kumaraswamy混合模型参数化路径，自动学习低方差的最优路径。
  - 在训练中联合优化得分函数与路径参数，实现端到端优化。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个复杂数据集上达到新的SOTA性能，显著提升模型准确性和稳定性。
  - 适用于各类基于得分的生成模型，如扩散模型、得分匹配等。
  - 无需人工设计路径，具有通用性和可扩展性。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次揭示路径方差是理论与实践差异的关键因素，提出可优化的完整目标函数。
  - 提出闭式路径方差表达式，使理论可落地。
  - 设计数据自适应路径学习机制，摆脱对人工路径设计的依赖。

title: Incorporating Expert Priors into Bayesian Optimization via Dynamic Mean Decay
abstract: Bayesian optimization (BO) is a powerful approach for black-box optimization, and in many real-world problems, domain experts possess valuable prior knowledge about promising regions of the search space. However, existing prior-informed BO methods are often overly complex, tied to specific acquisition functions, or highly sensitive to inaccurate priors. We propose DynMeanBO, a simple and general framework that incorporates expert priors into the Gaussian process mean function with a dynamic decay mechanism. This design allows BO to exploit expert knowledge in the early stages while gradually reverting to standard BO behavior, ensuring robustness against misleading priors while retaining the exploratory behavior of standard BO. DynMeanBO is broadly compatible with acquisition functions, introduces negligible computational cost, and comes with convergence guarantees under Expected Improvement and Upper Confidence Bound. Experiments on synthetic benchmarks and hyperparameter optimization tasks show that DynMeanBO accelerates convergence with informative priors and remains robust under biased ones.

title: Random-projection ensemble dimension reduction
abstract: We introduce a new, flexible, and theoretically justified framework for dimension reduction in high-dimensional regression, based on an ensemble of random projections. Specifically, we consider disjoint groups of independent random projections, retain the best projection in each group according to the empirical regression performance on the projected covariates, and then aggregate the selected projections via singular value decomposition. The singular values quantify the relative importance of corresponding projection directions and guide the dimension selection process. We investigate various aspects of our framework, including the choice of projection distribution and the number of projections used. Our theoretical results show that the expected estimation error decreases as the number of groups of projections increases. Finally, we demonstrate that our proposal consistently matches or outperforms state-of-the-art methods through extensive numerical studies on simulated and real data.

title: Rethinking Model Calibration through Spectral Entropy Regularization in Medical Image Segmentation
abstract: Deep neural networks for medical image segmentation often produce overconfident predictions, posing clinical risks due to miscalibrated uncertainty estimates. In this work, we rethink model calibration from a frequency-domain perspective and identify two critical factors causing miscalibration: spectral bias, where models overemphasize low-frequency components, and confidence saturation, which suppresses overall power spectral density in confidence maps. To address these challenges, we propose a novel frequency-aware calibration framework integrating spectral entropy regularization and power spectral smoothing. The spectral entropy term promotes a balanced frequency spectrum and enhances overall spectral power,  enabling better modeling of high-frequency boundary and low-frequency structural uncertainty. The smoothing module stabilizes frequency-wise statistics across training batches, reducing sample-specific fluctuations. Extensive experiments on six public medical imaging datasets and multiple segmentation architectures demonstrate that our approach consistently improves calibration metrics without sacrificing segmentation accuracy.

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统模型在医学图像分割中对边界和结构的不确定性建模不足，尤其在高频率细节区域容易误判，影响临床决策可靠性。
  - 现有校准方法多在空间域操作，忽视了频率域中模型对不同频段信息的偏差。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）

title: Risk-Sensitive Agent Compositions
abstract:
From software development to robot control, modern agentic systems decompose complex objectives into a sequence of subtasks and choose a set of specialized AI agents to complete them.
We formalize agentic workflows as directed acyclic graphs, called agent graphs, where edges represent AI agents and paths correspond to feasible compositions of agents.
Real-world deployment requires selecting agent compositions that not only maximize task success but also minimize violations of safety, fairness, and privacy requirements which demands a careful analysis of the low-probability (tail) behaviors of compositions of agents.
In this work, we consider risk minimization over the set of feasible agent compositions and seek to minimize the value-at-risk and the conditional value-at-risk of the loss distribution of the agent composition where the loss quantifies violations of these requirements.
We introduce an efficient algorithm which traverses the agent graph and finds a near-optimal composition of agents.
It uses a dynamic programming approach to approximate the value-at-risk of agent compositions by exploiting a union bound.
Furthermore, we prove that the approximation is near-optimal asymptotically for a broad class of practical loss functions.
We also show how our algorithm can be used to approximate the conditional value-at-risk as a byproduct.
To evaluate our framework, we consider a suite of video game-like control benchmarks that require composing several agents trained with reinforcement learning and demonstrate our algorithm's effectiveness in approximating the value-at-risk and identifying the optimal agent composition.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在复杂任务中选择多个AI智能体的组合，以同时最大化成功率并最小化安全、公平、隐私等高风险违规行为。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现实世界中的智能体系统（如机器人或软件）常需组合多个专用AI模块完成任务，但现有方法忽视了罕见但严重的“尾部风险”（如突发安全漏洞），可能导致灾难性后果。
  - 传统方法只关注平均性能，无法保障系统在极端情况下的可靠性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 将智能体组合建模为有向无环图（Agent Graph），路径代表可行的组合方案。
  - 提出一种基于动态规划的高效算法，利用“并集界”（union bound）近似计算组合的**风险值**（Value-at-Risk, VaR）和**条件风险值**（CVaR）。
  - 通过递推方式在图中遍历，逐步估算每条路径的高风险行为概率，从而筛选出低风险最优组合。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个类游戏控制任务中验证，算法能有效逼近真实VaR，识别出比传统方法更安全的智能体组合。
  - 可用于机器人控制、软件系统等需高可靠性的场景，尤其适合多智能体协作的复杂任务。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将**风险敏感优化**（risk-sensitive optimization）引入智能体组合问题，关注罕见但严重的违规行为。

title: Conformalized Survival Counterfactuals Prediction for General Right-Censored Data
abstract: This paper aims to develop a lower prediction bound (LPB) for survival time across different treatments in the general right-censored setting. Although previous methods have utilized conformal prediction to construct the LPB, their resulting prediction sets provide only probably approximately correct (PAC)–type miscoverage guarantees rather than exact ones. To address this problem, we propose a new calibration procedure under the potential outcome framework. Under the strong ignorability assumption, we propose a reweighting scheme that can transform the problem into a weighted conformal inference problem, allowing an LPB to be obtained via quantile regression with an exact miscoverage guarantee. Furthermore, our procedure is doubly robust against model misspecification. Empirical evaluations on synthetic and real-world clinical data demonstrate the validity and informativeness of our constructed LPBs, which indicate the potential of our analytical benchmark for comparing and selecting personalized treatments.

title: Robust Adversarial Quantification via Conflict-Aware Evidential Deep Learning
abstract: Reliability of deep learning models is critical for deployment in high-stakes applications, where out-of-distribution or adversarial inputs may lead to detrimental outcomes. Evidential Deep Learning, an efficient paradigm for uncertainty quantification, models predictions as Dirichlet distributions of a single forward pass. However, EDL is particularly vulnerable to adversarially perturbed inputs, making overconfident errors. Conflict-aware Evidential Deep Learning (C-EDL) is a lightweight post-hoc uncertainty quantification approach that mitigates these issues, enhancing adversarial and OOD robustness without retraining. C-EDL generates diverse, task-preserving transformations per input and quantifies representational disagreement to calibrate uncertainty estimates when needed. C-EDL's conflict-aware prediction adjustment improves detection of OOD and adversarial inputs, maintaining high in-distribution accuracy and low computational overhead. Our experimental evaluation shows that C-EDL significantly outperforms state-of-the-art EDL variants and competitive baselines, achieving substantial reductions in coverage for OOD data (up to $\approx55\%$) and adversarial data (up to $\approx90\%$), across a range of datasets, attack types, and uncertainty metrics.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何让深度学习模型在面对恶意或异常输入时仍能可靠地判断“自己不确定”？
- 动机：现有方法在对抗样本或分布外数据上容易过度自信，导致严重错误，需要更可靠的不确定性估计。
- 方法：提出C-EDL，通过生成多个相似但略有不同的输入（如轻微扰动），观察模型对这些输入的预测是否一致。若预测差异大（冲突），就认为模型不确定；反之则信任其输出。这是一种无需重新训练的“事后”增强方法。
- 结论：C-EDL在多种攻击和数据集上显著提升对异常输入的检测能力，减少错误置信度（覆盖），同时保持正常任务的准确率，计算开销极低。
- 创新点：首次将“预测冲突”作为不确定性信号，设计轻量级后处理机制，实现对对抗样本和分布外数据的鲁棒性增强。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何让深度学习模型在面对对抗样本或分布外数据时，能准确识别“自己不确定”，避免盲目自信。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有不确定性估计方法（如EDL）在恶意输入下容易产生过高置信度，导致严重误判，尤其在医疗、自动驾驶等高风险场景中后果严重。  
- 需要一种无需重新训练、轻量高效的方法来增强模型对异常输入的敏感性。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出C-EDL：对单个输入生成多个微小但任务一致的扰动版本（如轻微图像变形）。  
- 用同一模型分别预测这些扰动输入，计算预测结果之间的“冲突程度”（如类别分布差异）。  
- 若冲突大，则判定为不确定（可能是异常输入）；冲突小则信任输出。  
- 作为后处理模块，不改变原模型，仅增加少量推理开销。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在多种数据集（如CIFAR-10、ImageNet）和攻击类型（FGSM、PGD）下，C-EDL显著降低异常输入的错误置信度（覆盖下降达90%）。  
- 对分布外样本检测效果提升明显（覆盖减少约55%），同时保持正常任务高准确率。

title: Shrinking Proteins with Diffusion
abstract:
Many proteins useful in modern medicine or bioengineering are challenging to make in the lab, fuse with other proteins in cells, or deliver to tissues in the body because their sequences are too long.
Shortening these sequences typically involves costly, time-consuming experimental campaigns.
Ideally, we could instead use modern models of massive databases of sequences from nature to learn how to propose shrunken proteins that resemble sequences found in nature.
Unfortunately, these models struggle to efficiently search the combinatorial space of all deletions, and are not trained with inductive biases to learn how to delete.
To address this gap, we propose SCISOR, a novel discrete diffusion model that deletes letters from sequences to generate protein samples that resemble those found in nature.
To do so, SCISOR trains a de-noiser to reverse a forward noising process that adds random insertions to natural sequences.
As a generative model, SCISOR fits evolutionary sequence data competitively with previous large models.
In evaluation, SCISOR achieves state-of-the-art predictions of the functional effects of deletions on ProteinGym.
Finally, we use the SCISOR de-noiser to shrink long protein sequences, and show that its suggested deletions result in significantly more realistic proteins and more often preserve functional motifs than previous models of evolutionary sequences.
explain_zh:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何高效、准确地缩短蛋白质序列，同时保持其自然特性和功能。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 长蛋白质序列难以在实验室合成、融合或递送，传统缩短方法成本高、耗时长。
  - 现有模型缺乏有效搜索序列删除空间的能力，且未学习“如何合理删除”的规律。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出SCISOR：一种离散扩散模型，通过反向“去噪”过程逐步删除序列中的氨基酸。
  - 前向过程：向自然蛋白质序列中随机插入氨基酸，模拟“污染”。
  - 反向过程：训练一个去噪器学习从“被污染”序列中恢复原始序列，从而学会合理删除。
  - 生成时，从随机序列出发，逐步去噪并删除，生成更短但自然的蛋白质。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）

title: Amortising Inference and Meta-Learning Priors in Neural Networks
abstract: One of the core facets of Bayesianism is in the updating of prior beliefs in light of new evidence$\textemdash$so how can we maintain a Bayesian approach if we have no prior beliefs in the first place? This is one of the central challenges in the field of Bayesian deep learning, where it is not clear how to represent beliefs about a prediction task by prior distributions over model parameters. Bridging the fields of Bayesian deep learning and probabilistic meta-learning, we introduce a way to $\textit{learn}$ a weights prior from a collection of datasets by introducing a way to perform per-dataset amortised variational inference. The model we develop can be viewed as a neural process whose latent variable is the set of weights of a BNN and whose decoder is the neural network parameterised by a sample of the latent variable itself. This unique model allows us to study the behaviour of Bayesian neural networks under well-specified priors, use Bayesian neural networks as flexible generative models, and perform desirable but previously elusive feats in neural processes such as within-task minibatching or meta-learning under extreme data-starvation.

  - 现有方法难以在小样本或数据稀缺场景下有效进行贝叶斯推理。

title: Causal Score Conditioning for Multi-Resolution Latent Systems
abstract: Complex causal systems with interdependent variables require inference from heterogeneous observations that vary in spatial resolution, temporal frequency, and noise characteristics due to data acquisition constraints. Existing multi-modal fusion approaches assume uniform data quality or complete observability -- assumptions often violated in real-world applications. Current methods face three limitations: they treat causally-related variables independently, failing to exploit causal relationships; they cannot integrate multi-resolution observations effectively; and they lack theoretical frameworks for cascaded approximation errors. We introduce the Score-based Variational Graphical Diffusion Model (SVGDM), which integrates score-based diffusion within causal graphical structures for inference under heterogeneous incomplete observations. SVGDM introduces causal score decomposition enabling information propagation across causally-connected variables while preserving original observation characteristics. Diffusion provides a natural way to model scale-dependent sensing noise, which is common in remote-sensing, climate, and physical measurement systems, while the causal graph encodes well-established mechanistic dependencies between latent processes. We provide theoretical analysis and demonstrate superior performance on both synthetic and real-world datasets compared to relevant baselines.
explain_zh:
- 问题：如何在观测数据分辨率、频率、噪声不一致的情况下，准确推断复杂因果系统中的隐藏变量？
- 动机：真实世界的数据（如气象、遥感）常因设备限制导致观测质量参差不齐，现有方法无法有效融合这些异质数据，且忽略变量间的因果关系。
- 方法：提出SVGDM模型，将扩散模型与因果图结合，通过“因果得分分解”实现跨变量信息传递，同时保留原始观测的分辨率和噪声特征；利用扩散过程建模不同尺度的传感噪声。
- 结论：在合成与真实数据上均优于基线方法，尤其在低分辨率或高噪声场景下表现更优，适用于遥感、气候建模等多尺度物理系统。
- 创新点：首次将扩散模型与因果图结构结合，提出因果得分分解机制，建立多分辨率观测下的误差传播理论框架。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 在观测数据分辨率、时间频率和噪声水平不一致的情况下，如何准确推断复杂因果系统中的隐藏变量。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 真实世界数据（如气象、遥感）常因设备限制导致观测质量差异大，现有方法要么假设数据质量一致，要么忽略变量间的因果关系，难以应对实际挑战。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出SVGDM模型：将扩散模型嵌入因果图结构中；  
- 引入“因果得分分解”机制，让信息在因果关联变量间有效传递；  
- 扩散过程自然建模多尺度传感噪声，保留原始观测的分辨率与噪声特征；  
- 通过变分推断实现对不完整、异质观测的联合推理。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在合成与真实数据集上均显著优于现有基线方法；  
- 特别在低分辨率或高噪声条件下表现更稳健；  
- 适用于遥感、气候建模等存在多尺度观测的物理系统。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将扩散模型与因果图结构结合，实现因果驱动的多分辨率推理；  
- 提出“因果得分分解”新机制，支持跨变量信息融合；  
- 建立多分辨率观测下的误差传播理论框架，填补理论空白。
